reviews.title: Stay away from this place
reviews.text:
The hotel was a joke. Old, smelly and crowded. Bathroom was not clean (especially shower curtains). Most of the staff has no idea what good service is. The room had 1 fork and 1 plate. Asking for a wine bottle opener involved 3 people and a staff member who eventually arrived to open the bottle and refused to leave the opener (as they "didn't have enough"). I mean, 5 star hotel?! Seriously?

The "upgraded" room was connected to another room (!) and you could hear everything what was going on behind the door that was next to the bed. Just like living in a dorm. 

Food was really unsatisfactory together with restaurant service during meal times when staff rudely interrupts your breakfast asking random questions like: "Room number please?", "Can I take this plate?" (as you actually eat your food from the very plate), etc. If you are a "5 star" hotel - do a better job at NOT asking my room number 3-5 times a day for no particular reason.

To top it up - be prepared for hidden costs. Everything +10-20% of some weird taxes and service fees on your bills.

On the "bright" side - the view was nice, but at the cost of constant noise from the streets (though if you close the balcony door and turn the 20-year-old aircon on - you can change the noise to the one the aircon makes). 

There is also no private beach whatsoever. 

Stay away from this place.

reviews.title: Horrible customer service and inhospitality
reviews.text: "It's 11pm o'clock now, your guest has to leave NOW!", with a threatening attitude. My 82 years old mom was frightened the entire night. The Vietnam tourism authority had provided me the contact info to report my case later. When we checked in at 7pm, I advised we'll have 3 guests instead of 2, thinking it's ok as the document states capacity is for 3. They asked to pay for the extra person & suggested me to contact w/Agoda the next day. I said I would after clarifying w/Agoda. The very next morning we barely sat down for breakfast, the Ass. Manager harassed me saying "Our security camera seeing you have 3 person in the room, you have to come & pay NOW!"  We had paid all 3 Club suites for 4 nights in advance in full, plus they ran my credit card for 5M VDN when we checked in. Why they had to harass guest like that in short arrival who basically had paid for everything? We did pay for the extra persons which was more expensive by adding a room but they claimed they didn't have one.

reviews.title: Never come back Sunrise hotel
reviews.text: We booked via Agoda to stay at Sunrise hotel for one night for a Club Suite Queen Bed room and an extra bed for my kid. For the readiness of the extra bed, I had already called the hotel reception 30min before arriving at the hotel, but the extra bed was not available when getting in the room around 02:00am in the morning. We were so tired after a long drive to Nha Trang and disappointed with the room readiness. The receptionisted confirmed about the readiness of the extra bed when I called him but it was not ready. I was so mad at that time. Then we had to wait for 45min to set up the extra bed in my room. We went to bed at 3 o'clock in the morning. On the other hand, the hotel facilities are old and not well-maintained such as broken buttons on AC controller, dirty carpets. Besides, the breakfast buffet there was terrible. The food was yucky and little. To sum up, I do not mind to spend money on 5 star hotels and services but I was extremely disappointed at Sunrise hotel. It's not a 5 star in my opinion. I will never go there again. We moved to another 5 star hotel in the next day.

reviews.title: threatened by staff
reviews.text: generally the hotel is very nice, however gym could you a little upgrade. the main issue was with the supervisors behavior. My girlfriend and i arrived in different time for a getaway weekend. on the second day the hotel staff asked for my girlfriend's passport but forgotten her passport at home which was about 20-30mins away from the hotel. The hotel supervisor called me and threatened to call the police right away. we both went down, that is when we were rudely told to go home and get it, I (not supervisor!) had offer that we can show a photocopy of it, this turned out to be a solution. My girlfriend and i are both expats in Vietnam, we never came across such behavior from hotels and getting embarrassed publicly. it is unbelievable that staff threaten customers right away for a lack of passport dont try to think of solutions. and when we fail to comply she threaten to call police or we check  out. i would have checked out if i wouldnt have prepaid for all this. Dear Agoda, if there is more to be done against such behavior and staff please let me know i am willing to take any necessary steps so other customers dont get treated like criminals in a 5 star hotel for absolutely no reason

reviews.title: Very Disappointing for a 5-star hotel
reviews.text:
Overall disappointing for a assumed 5-star hotel.
The pictures and description of the hotel are quite misleading as the hotel is not directly at the beach but separated by a very busy noisy road. The assumed private beach opposite the road has a single row of sun beds. It’s part of the open beach front and open to vendors and locals. There is no beach bar and drinks are "warm" served from a cool box and towels out of a big bag. No relaxed private beach feeling as everybody is staring at you from the boulevard above you while enjoying the noisy street and sun disappears behind the building after 16.00   
The rooms have big beds with very poor thick pillows. The bathroom was smelling and the bath tub had mould. Hot water was not working well and interior was damaged. The volt/safe just fits your passport forcing you to leave valuables outside. The swimming pool was good and great pool service. There were very small umbrellas though and sun disappears after 15.30. The Mattress was quite hard for a full day at the pool. 
The breakfast was good though not offering any orange juice. The was staff was quite rude and often limited available especially for a hotel assumed 5-star. Many times nobody at service desk, front door or answering calls.
Our overall experience was that the hotel was disappointing and definitely not setting a 5-star standard and therefore overpriced for it's quality and offering. In addition the hotel is quite far from restaurants and bars and where it's happening.
In our opinion and other guests we spoke during our stay the hotel is heavily overrated in the reviews and not understood why. 
To our opinion there are much better options available and new hotels being build although in general I would stay away from noicy Nha Trang and go to Hoi An. This for true relaxing 5* quality and private white sand beaches unless you are looking for nightlife.
reviews.positives: Location not great No true private beach

We spent NYE at the hotel and enjoyed the festivities with a great live band, and a lobster dinner that was above and beyond!

The location was wonderful. 

Highly recommend!  We'll be back!

reviews.title: Nice looking hotel, great staff but unclean rooms
reviews.text:
I stayed here a number of years ago when it just opened.  Didn't like the layout of the room too much.  The room is long and narrow with a small bathroom.  Shower is not separated from bathtub.  Stayed this time because the price was reasonable.  

This is a beautiful hotel looking from the outside. Staff is nice as usual (been here several times for buffet dinners).  The hallway and rooms show signs of wear and tear which need a major renovation. The room smells funny (damp, old) which gives a feeling of a dirty room.  Elevator smells food, garlic...  Furniture, bedsheets, and carpet are too old.

We liked buffet dinner here and came several times in the past. This time the food was not as good and not many selections.

Overall, it is not even a 4 stars hotel.  Wouldn't recommend.

reviews.title: Not the best hotel we've stayed at.
reviews.text:
It probably was a nice hotel a few years ago, a little tired looking right now. The pro's were the breakfast was very nice and the pool area. 
The cons were the AC never got to an acceptable temperature. We had to leave it on and still never dropped  below 24°. The hot water took an absolute age to come through. We did complain to the front desk but no real action was taken. Towels were like sand paper, very thin and cheap, hardly bath size. 
I know the country is struggling with the current health issues as every country is but I'd struggle to see there being enough facilities on the beach and around the pool if there was a normal tourist trade.

reviews.title: Great hotel, but bad location (and don't get the airport transfer you pay for...)
reviews.text:
This hotel is beautiful, the room we were in (Junior Ocean Suite) was a nice size, with a lovely Jacuzzi on the balcony. The swimming pool was lovely as well, a nice roman style round pool. My biggest problem is probably Nha Trang itself. The beach was filthy, littered with trash. The hotel is right by a big road, and while it didn't bother us in the room, as we were on the top floor, as soon as you leave the hotel, the noise and pollution hits you. 
And lastly, we took advantage of their airport transfer service both way, splashing out money at a private car at the increased cost, only to be greeted by a shuttle bus. We were the only passengers, but the 40 minute ride to the hotel would've been much more comfortable in a car. The same happened when we were leaving, despite having paid more, and the hotel had a car available parked right outside, we still were shuffled into a shuttle bus. Again, no other passengers. It was a rip-off. So while the room was nice, we'll not be coming back

Also found the hotel to feel like a bit of a ghost town despite going at peak season. The pool, the bar... quite often close to empty. They all must have been at the beach as you'd have to wait for an umbrella most days. At the beach and the breakfast buffet we seemed to be surrounded by a lot of tiny-swimsuit clad Russians... bit of an unexpected culture shock! Had some damn good cocktails in the rooftop bar but it was a bit of a Lost in Translation experience with a Vietnamese musician playing American songs to a crowd of about 4 people. Could be better if they improved the atmosphere and weren't so concerned with wooing the Russian mafia!

reviews.title: Good location and service
reviews.text:
The Sunrise is starting to show its age. The towels are a bit old and grey, but still a good hotel. A bit overpriced now that things are getting older. A few kilometres from the main tourist cafes, the Sunrise is still a great location. The quality of rooms does vary and you get what you pay for. The lobby is dark and gloomy and the staff are obviously trying hard to smile, but its hard to maintain when it is not too busy and things get a bit boring.

The service is good. The breakfast buffet is great for the business traveller. But he hotel is a little overpriced compared to other very nice hotels nearby. Vietnamese hotels are great value - so give the American chains a miss a go for something local. The Sunrise should be $70 dollars per night or $80 for a deluxe room.

reviews.title: Great staff poor management
reviews.text:
We booked the Seaview deluxe room, but ensure you get a high floor room, otherwise you will be overlooking the pool area and concrete, flooring. Rooms are very spacious and room service was excellent, with good quality food and quick delivery.
Breakfast buffet was not hot, and although we mentioned it to staff, there were no managers on the floor, it remained the same for our 5 day stay. Hotel is a traditional one but will need updating of facilities as they are showing age, many new modern hotels have state of the art equipment and surroundings.
We had to pay an extra $20 per day to use the gym and sauna facilities which is odd as most 5* hotels offer this for free, especially as we upgraded our room and was paying over $150 pn.
Evenings were empty and no atmosphere, all the bars and restaurants were deserted, even with a full hotel, we were there for Tet the Lunar New Year. They could improve offers to entice guests to stay in their hotel.
Staff were very friendly and helpful but the running of the hotel can be improved by management, starting by being visible to hotel guests.

reviews.title: Für den Preis OK aber nicht nochmal! Keine Erwartungen haben, dann gehts vielleicht?
reviews.positives: Das Personal und das Frühstück sind top! Alle MitarbeiterInnen sind wirklich sehr nett und bemüht soweit man das in Vietnam eben erwarten kann. Frühstück ist auch sehr umfangreich und frisch. Da findet sich jeder etwas.
reviews.negatives: Leider ist das Hotel sehr in die Jahre gekommen, extreme Wasserschäden im ganzen Hotel, in den Gängen ist Teppichboden der so dermaßen stinkt weil der Boden anscheinend auch bereits nass ist und wie es scheint nur gesaugt wird. Der Boden ist wie er aussieht seit Eröffnung Anfang der 2000er Jahre im Hotel, da kann man sich vorstellen wie der aussieht und richt. Ekelhaft. Die Zimmer sind absolut abgewohnt und NICHT sauber, ich habe als erstes das Zimmer selbst Grundgereinigt! Das Putzwasser war schwarz! Die Badewanne mit Whirlpool Funktion war so dreckig, daher habe ich diese zwar gereinigt aber nie verwendet! Leider geht vor dem Hotel eine vierspurige Straße vorbei, die sehr sehr stark befahren ist, Lärm, extreme Abgase und dauerndes Hupen! Unglaublich eigentlich! Leider ist auch das zum Hotel gehörende Bistro/Restaurant/ Pavillon, nur bis 22:00 Uhr offen und es ist als fotohotspot für einheimische junge Damen und Mädchen sehr beliebt, die sich auch gleich dort herrichten und somit ist alles voller Haare, die Damen sind mit glätteisen, bürsten und Kosmetik ausgestattet und verdrecken alles, im Innenraum des Pavillons kann man sich nicht hinsetzen da alles voller Haare ist. Ebenfalls ekelhaft! Der Strand ist leider voller Müll und es räumt auch niemand weg ganz im Gegenteil, es wirft einfach jeder alles weg wo er steht und liegt. Stört ja auch niemanden, bis auf aus. Spa ist nicht mehr offen, und Fitnessraum ist ebenfalls mit dem stinkenden Teppichboden ausgestattet. Im allgemeinen alles sehr verwaist und extrem abgewohnt Wir sind zwei Wochen früher abgereist. Nicht zu empfehlen. Aber für den Preis in Ordnung, wenn man keine Erwartungen hat.

reviews.text:
There are many cheaper hotels in Nha Trang but it is all about how you want to travel. We typically aim on the higher end of the scale and this hotel met it.

The hotel has 2 wings with the suites and bigger rooms in the Annex building. It's a little confusing at first but nothing you can't get over.

We never made it to the pool since we were out and abouts a lot but it did appear to be nice. The breakfast was on par with over hotels on our vacation.

The suits come with some benefits like free drinks (minus alcohol), free coffee/tea time plus cake slice, and free drinks plus alcohol from 21:00 - 23:00.

The spa was worth the money. A bit high for VN but the girls were great and honest.

reviews.title: Nice hotel close to the beach
reviews.text:
Nice (but not perfect) room
+Nice bathroom
-Very small balcony (not big enough to put a chair on even)
Location of hotel is nice, you only need to cross the road to get to the private beach (there is staff to make sure to stop the trafic for you). On the beach there is staff walking around talking orders for food and drinks if you like something while you relax in you sunbed. The walk to the center of night life takes 10-15min (~1.5km) and to the harbour (where the popular daily tours depart are ~5km away (you need a taxi)
The hotel is large with multiple restaurants (all quite expensive compared to Vitnamese standards). Especially the japaniese restaurant was very good. The irish pub had very loud music so not many people endoured more than a short time. At the hotel it is possible to arrange al types of trips but these trips can also be arranged at small stands/shops downtown. The difference is that the hotel can arrange more luxerious trips while the stands/stands can arrange cheaper trips.
The lobby is nice but the corridors in other places of the hotel felt like they needed a bit of updating.
Overall a nice stay.
reviews.positives: Close to the beach, walking distance to the center of Nah Trang

The food was good, but a little on the expensive side (54.000 dong for a small local beer, 150.000 for a salad).

reviews.title: Service with a smile & friendly staff
reviews.text: The Sunrise Hotel is a great hotel for a stay in Nha Trang. Although some of the rooms look slightly out-dated and the carpets equally so, the overall impression of the hotel is one of comfort and a great place to base your stay in Nha Trang. There's a grand piano in the lobby and those customers who are pianists are able to tinkle the ivories and show off their skills. The hotel looks across the sea and has a perfect vantage point for the spectacular early morning sunrises (5am) which must be seen at least once in any stay at the Sunrise.

Die Lage war perfekt, natürlich hört man den Straßenlärm, aber den hört man in Nha Trang wahrscheinlich überall, aber man hört auch den ganzen Tag das Meeresrauschen 😍 Vor allem ist das Hotel, gegenüber anderen Betonklötzen, überschaubar und hat einen Grand Hotel Charme! Der Pool ist perfekt mit relativ vielen Liegen und hat relativ lange Sonne. Auch die zwei Herren beim Pool, wie das ganze Hotelpersonal, angefangen von der Rezeption bis zum Frühstücksservice sind sehr freundlich und bemüht! Die Zimmer sind groß, alle zum Strand, der nur durch die Straße getrennt ist. Meines hatte einen Whirlpool am Balkon. Frühstück mit großer Auswahl, viel asiatisch, auch europäisch und eigener Eier- und Suppenstation.
Eigenen Strandbereich gibt es keinen, aber es gibt auf diesem um geringes Geld Liegen zu mieten.
reviews.negatives: Gefallen hat mir alles, natürlich ist es nicht auf dem modernsten Stand, aber das positive wiegt das auf!

reviews.title: Lovely Stay
reviews.text: The stay here is very relaxing.  It is a mid-range luxury hotel that seems popular with families and foreign business people.  The rooms are spacious, and most importantly, all afford gorgeous views of the Bay.  The hotel is modern enough, not overly charming, not totally soul-less.  The staff all speak good english, are very professional and provide good information. The restaurant for the morning buffet is extremely pleasant and offers an international and vietnamese menu, all passible.  The local market is within walking distance.  For a comfortable stay with all the desired services, Sunrise provides good value.
reviews.positives: Facility, staff, proximity to beach, lobby, the best point in the view of the ocean; there is classical piano music in the evening.
reviews.negatives: The top floor bar was not so busy, but this is ok as there are room there.  The spa seems over priced but there are plenty of options in town

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.positives: È la struttura ideale per chi, come noi, non vuole rinchiudersi in uno dei mille resort nuovi presenti in questa zona. Al contrario dei resort, che sono spesso vuoti ed isolati e con ristoranti semi chiusi, il Sunrise è vivace, ha un ristorante ottimo, una piscina comoda e grande, una vista dell' oceano incantevole. Il personale è formato e cordiale, le camere sono comode, spaziose e vendono pulite con cura ogni giorno. Si affaccia sul lungomare di nha trang. Lo stile classico della struttura ci è piaciuto molto. La colazione è ricca e soddisfa gusti internazionali.

The Bar on the 10th Floor lacked atmosphere and did not attract guests which was a shame as it had a lovely view.

reviews.title: Nice hotel
reviews.text:
This hotel was very nice with great customer service. The views from the balcony were beautiful. The breakfast buffet was very good and of a high standard.
For its age it was very nice with great character. Good pool and also opposite the beach where the hotel provide beach chairs.
The room was big and and the bed very comfy.
Nha Trang itself held little appeal as it was not really geared to the tourists, we really walked around to find a restaurant to eat in and there was not much on offer at the markets for shopping.
We had 3 days in Nha Trang hotel was great but i won't return as it held little interest for me. If you love the hype in Patong then don't visit Nha Trang.

reviews.title: Lovely beach stop
reviews.text:
Arrived around 9pm and the check in was well handled and fairly quick.  Taken to room and shown how things worked.  Found that one of the restaurants was shut during our stay but this did not affect us in any way.  Except for one lunch only ate breakfast at hotel.  Breakfast was very good with a nice selection of European and Asian dishes.  Various egg dishes prepared as you wait.  Had issues with WIFI throughout stay.  Several sites would not open and others were slow.  Have had no problems in other places that we have been in Vietnam.  Booked 2 tours with Tour Desk but only went on one due to poor evening weather.  As with most 5 Star hotels tours are more expensive than booked outside.  The tour to a waterfall was good but lunch was poor.

The hotel is across a busy road from a very nice beach.  Due to afternoon winds had limited opportunities to use it.

All members of staff had good English skills and were very helpful.

Despite negative comments we really enjoyed our stay in this lovely hotel
reviews.positives: The room was lovely with plenty of space and a nice bathroom.  Great views from the good size balcony.  Good selection of food for breakfast.

reviews.title: 나트랑 최고의 호텔 선택!
reviews.positives:
이름처럼 해가 뜨는 것이 잘 보이는 호텔이었어요 새벽 5시에 일어나 해뜨는 걸 보면서 하루를 시작할 수 있었어요. 

새벽 비행기라 얼리체크인 신청했는데 1시부터 입실 가능하다고 했지만, 리셉션에서 조금더 유동적으로 추가금 없이 11시 입실 해줘서 더욱 편하게 있을 수 있었어요. 객실 업그레이드도 해줘서 넓~은 객실에서 편하게 쉴 수 있었습니다. 무조건 추천합니다! 

수영장도 꽤 깊어서 좋았고, 크기가 작을까봐 걱정했는데 생각보다 사람들이 사용을 많이 안해서 넓고 편하게 쓸 수 있었어요. 수영 후 수영복 말릴 수 있는 건조대도 베란다에 있어서 좋았고 사용하진 않았지만 베란다에 스파욕조 있는 것도 꼭 쓰고 싶었어요. 

웰컴 과일과 칼이 있어서 편하게 망고 까먹을 수 있었고, 과일을 사다 놓았더니 청소하시는 분이 보고 과도도 주셔서 세심한 배려가 느껴졌습니다. 짐을 맡기는 사람이 많은지 체크 아웃할 때 먼저 물어보시는 것도 인상적이었습니다. 

전반적으로 만족스러운 호텔이었어요. (수압도 좋고 뜨거운 물도 엄청 잘나와요)
reviews.negatives: 특별히 없었습니다. 제공되는 실내화가 쪼리라 평소에 선호하지 않는 사람들은 개인 실내화를 챙기면 좋을 것 같아요. 위치가 좋아서 시내 어디를 가도 그랩 기본요금으로 갈 수 있어요. 아! 일요일에 수영장에서 결혼식해서 사용할 수 있는지는 모르겠어요. 호핑 투어로 인해 수영장 사용 계획이 없었어서 상관 없었지만 미리 알면 더 좋을 것 같아요. 객실 베란다에서 외국 결혼식 구경해서 신기했어요. 이것조차 좋았습니다.

The pool is really nice too.  The hotel also let's you use the private beach out the front with sunbeds for no charge. 
The hotel rooms are large and have a balcony that looks towards the beach.

reviews.text:
this hotel was simply fantastic. it won “best hotel in vietnam” in 2009 and it’s not hard to see why.
the price was good when compared to other 5-star offerings. 
the hotel is located on the esplanade of the northern end of the main beach; about 20 minutes walk from the most popular bars, restaurants and nightclubs (such as the sailing club which seems to be “the” spot). 
the walk to the main area is pleasant enough though as it is all paved. recently though nha trang has been going seriously downhill and there’s many reports of muggings. i personally didn’t have any problems but i’m 2m and 100kg, so would recommend going by taxi if coming back late. it’s definitely a viper pit in the downtown party area at the moment (but that’s another story).
the staff and service at the hotel were excellent. extremely friendly and helpful as you would expect for a 5-star hotel. chocolates were left every evening, and a fruit basket every morning.
the hotel facilities and room were fantastic. the room was cleaned and re-stocked very regularly, at least twice per day. we stayed in a deluxe room, all of which have an outdoor jacuzzi on the balcony. it appears most if not all rooms for the hotel have private balconies and face out on to the sea. can’t be 100% sure of that though.
the gym was pretty good. great sound system, a few rowers, ellipticals, exercise bikes and a good multi-machine. free weights were fly-lock dumbbells so can adjust as required.
buffet breakfast was excellent. not quite the variety as some 5-star offerings, but definitely up to a 5-star standard, being made extremely well and plenty of choices. and the setting is beautiful. nice big french style area and a really lovely way to start the day.
there’s free computers and wi-fi throughout the hotel, including in bedrooms. sometimes it was pretty patchy and slow. not sure if that was a hotel thing or an internet thing generally as it has been patchy in hanoi and everywhere in recent weeks.
reviews.positives: view, ambience, setting for breakfast

reviews.title: Koreans who are about to travel to Nha Trang should definitely see this.
reviews.text:
I hope this helps Korean travelers deciding on accommodations in Nha Trang.

- Location

The location is actually a bit far from the center of Nha Trang. There are other hotels and hotel facilities nearby, but you will need to walk over 30 minutes to reach popular restaurants listed on Google. The advantage is that the walking path along the beach keeps the view interesting. It’s nice for a stroll; however, the downside is that the weather in Nha Trang is extremely hot and humid, so by the time you reach the city center, you will likely be drenched in sweat. Please keep in mind that taking a taxi like Grab could be quite costly.

- Hygiene

Although it appears to be an older hotel, the hygiene is not bad. The rooms are cleaned well every day, and there is no dust or pests. However, the filter on the showerhead in the bathroom changed slightly yellow after just one day, and there was a lot of black powder like debris under the sink. When we inquired at the desk, they said they would clean it right away, but no one came until we checked out. The sink also had issues with drainage; when we reported it, it seemed to be fixed temporarily but eventually returned to its original state. It could be possible that the room we used simply had poor conditions (we booked a premium deluxe ocean view room with twin beds).

- Staff

The staff were very friendly and helpful from the moment we checked in until we checked out. When calling a taxi at the hotel, the staff would come out to carry our luggage and flag down the taxi without us even asking. We asked the front desk staff many questions, and they always responded warmly and attentively. When there was a language barrier with Vietnamese, they even made calls on our behalf and did their best to help with any requests.

- Service

As mentioned well on the site, this hotel offers airport pick-up and drop-off, laundry services, and luggage storage after check-out. However, using Klook for airport transfers is cheaper than directly through the hotel. The hotel laundry service is not particularly expensive, but you won't be able to receive it back the same day or within a few hours; it takes at least 1-3 days. If you need laundry done quickly, I recommend using a nearby laundromat. The luggage storage after check-out is especially useful for those with later departure times, and they keep it securely without missing any items, so you can rest easy on that front.

++ Personal Review
The hotel itself has a European style and is beautiful with a luxurious white-tone interior, so those who believe “a beautiful hotel that photographs well is the best!” will not regret making a reservation here.

Many of you might bring your own showerhead filters when staying at hotels in Vietnam. In the case of Sunrise Hotel, I'm not sure if it's due to the age of the building’s facilities, but we accidentally dropped the showerhead after attaching the filter, and it came apart at the joint with the filter, resulting in a compensation fee of 500,000 dong. If you plan to change the filter, please be careful not to drop or break the showerhead.

reviews.title: Good Hotel and Value for Money
reviews.text: The hotel is located just in front of the beach and it is just a 5 minutes walking distance to the Nha Trang Center shopping mall.   We booked for the Premier Ocient View room,  the view from the balcony is fantastic. However, there are two types of this room, if you want a jacuzzi in your balcony, you have to mention it in your booking which we only knew this after checked in. The services and buffet breakfast are good. The front desk ladies are friendly and helpful and everything is satisfied.  However the Gym room and kids club are located in different gaming club building which is connected from level one. We found the peoples are smoking in the lift and caused smelly in the lobby. Hope the hotel can strictly enforce a non-smoking policy. Furthermore, the kids club is small and found there is nothing much to play for the kids (if you bring your kids there). Overall it is still recommended and value for money to stay here for your  Nha Trang holiday.

reviews.title: Very Nice and Clean
reviews.text: This was my first time to Nha Trang, I have no expectation and just went through Agoda for hotel. Then came across Sunrise and it didn't disappoint me. When we reach there, I felt glad by looking at the bright and spacious lobby. It well located in the main and busiest Tran Phu Street. Upon checking in, receptionist told me that my room is facing the seas (maybe all the room facing sea?). The room design some how is very European and it come with a see through bath room (don't worry, it has blind as well). Our room come with breakfast and the breakfast is delicious and many choices. The beach is 2 min walking distance across the road (all new hotel located across the beach as per the local). we have no trouble finding food and drink, all within walking distance.  However, if the Government consider the center of Tran Phu Stree, Sunrise is bit far end from this building(also walking distance though). If you are OK to take cab to the hottest spot in Tran Phu Street, namely. Sailing Club and Louisiana, then Sunrise will be perfect for you. If I ever go to Nha Trang again, will try to stay near to those spots.  Overall, I am very satisfy with Sunrise.
